heading: 1. Maintenance Release Plan > 1.1 Project Overview:
content_sample: HAL 4.0 MR1 is a maintenance release to address findings from the preclinical evaluation, customer facing issues and backlog items. This program is not intended to include items driven by new, modified, or deleted requirements or features and will NOT require a 510K submission.  The changes introduced are software only and do not impact hardware. The additional scope for TRR2 is highlighted below in Table 1, which also presents a comprehensive list of the entire program scope. The added scope falls into 3 main categories: 1. SW Bugs reported by the Investigational Use Institutions or found during TRR1 V&V - if unsolved, these issues will very likely trigger customer complaints Issue 1312448 Strong artifact in clinical images with XL imager RTI8643iL [Investigational Use Finding] Issue 1311681 Migration of CBCT modes: the Iterative_Acuros reconstructions are not available for IGRT modes [TRR1 V&V] 1231691 CBCT Reconstructor Image wrong workstation name [TRR1 V&V]

heading: 3. AUMC features to support Customer Tenders
content_sample: Additionally, a business request to support Amsterdam University Medical Center (AUMC) features required an additional scope update. These features were originally scoped for HAL4.0 MR2 which has recently been canceled. -  1276583 Bore Collision Offset, this feature will be added to the Service Advanced Rights. This will improve serviceability for the Maintenance Technician during preventative maintenance. If the Maintenance Technician has this right, they will be able to recapture the bore collision offset in Service Mode after the reinstallation of the bore cover. This feature is currently only available for Varian Service via HASP protection. HASP access will be maintained for Varian Service. - 1276585 MV and kV offset, offset flatness, and gain values in Machine Performance Check HAL 4.0 MR1 Maintenance Release Document This document contains confidential, proprietary information of Varian Medical Systems. heading: 29 1276585  Customer Tenders (MVP, AUMC) - View only view of MPC > 1.5 Software Configuration Management
content_sample: The Software Configuration Management process in this section will be defined by the Development Operations team (DevOps@varian.com) during Elaboration. Infrastructure, Repositories, and Branching Strategy are defined in the sections following. For HAL 4.0, we have migrated all the console, embedded, NLS, and system builds from legacy XAML builds into TFS Azure Build Pipelines (vNext). For HAL 4.0, we will be providing a centralized deployment tool that would upgrade all console software from a centralized location. Formal builds will be available at: \\pa-netapp10\pa_tfsbuild\HAL Released software will be in Edgecast: https://pse.oscs.varian.com/

heading: 2. Requirements > 3.2 Risk Analysis
content_sample: All the changes described in this document and their effects on the system, including external interfaces, were analyzed to assess whether any new hazards needed to be created or the risks associated with any existing hazards changed. HAL 4.0 MR1 is a maintenance release that address findings from the preclinical evaluation, customer facing issues and backlog items. None of these findings were determined to cause harm to the patient or user. No new hazards were identified, and no existing risks changed as a result of this release. heading: 5. Verification and Validation > 5.1 Verification and Validation Plan > 5.1.6 Verification and Validation Activities > 5.1.6.1 V&V Approach > 5.1.6.1.2 Risk and Hazard Testing
content_sample: No new risks or risk control measures were identified, and no existing risks or risk control measures changed as a result of this release. Therefore, no new safety test cases were created, and no existing test cases were updated for verification. Existing safety related requirements were chosen to be re-verified based on the impact analysis.

heading: 5. Verification and Validation > 5.1 Verification and Validation Plan > 5.1.6 Verification and Validation Activities > 5.1.6.1 V&V Approach > 5.1.6.1.3 Regression Testing
content_sample: Existing HAL test cases will be selected to run based on the impact analysis with regard to the changes made in HAL 4.0 MR1 including 4.0 MR1 development, Issue fixes, merge with True Beam, and compatibility with updates to ARIA and Eclipse. All safety and risk controls will be tested as a part of verification unless justification is provided. Regression analysis will be performed based on an opt in methodology, whereby requirements affected by HAL 4.0 MR1 will be identified along with a justification why regression testing is necessary. Linked test cases to those requirements will then be included in the HAL 4.0 MR1 regression test suite; all other HAL test cases will not be run for the HAL 4.0 MR1 release.

heading: 5. Verification and Validation > 5.1 Verification and Validation Plan > 5.1.6 Verification and Validation Activities > 5.1.6.1 V&V Approach > 5.1.6.1.4 Test Automation
content_sample: HAL 4.0 MR1 will utilize automation testing for stability testing and formal requirements testing.

heading: 5. Verification and Validation > 5.1 Verification and Validation Plan > 5.1.6 Verification and Validation Activities > 5.1.6.1 V&V Approach > 5.1.6.1.4 Test Automation > 5.1.6.1.4.1 Stability Testing
content_sample: HAL test automation framework will be used to perform Halcyon ARIA stability testing, functional testing, Ethos adaptive workflow testing and machine stability automation. For automation, the delivery of various imaging and treatment techniques in clinical mode will be executed by an automation test framework. As per HAL current usage model approximately 100 patients are treated every day – where automation framework will be used to deliver 200 treatments to capture the performance counters. Note that the results of these automated tests are recorded outside of TFS, as they are not part of the formal requirements testing. HAL 4.0 MR1 Maintenance Release Document This document contains confidential, proprietary information of Varian Medical Systems. It may not be copied or reproduced without prior written permission from Varian Medical Systems. Doc Number Rev. Date D69533 05 Date of last e-Sign DND01F02 Rev 01 Page 28 of 34

heading: 5. Verification and Validation > 5.1 Verification and Validation Plan > 5.1.6 Verification and Validation Activities > 5.1.6.1 V&V Approach > 5.1.6.1.4 Test Automation > 5.1.6.1.4.1 Stability Testing (Continued)
content_sample: The above automation test runs are documented by a summary report, which includes a description of the plans used for the runs, the number of sessions treated and the number of sessions that were not treated. This report also includes faults, warnings and errors that occurred and the disposition of these anomalies. The summary report should contain at least 100 hours of run time for each of the machines that automation is run on.

heading: 5. Verification and Validation > 5.1 Verification and Validation Plan > 5.1.6 Verification and Validation Activities > 5.1.6.1 V&V Approach > 5.1.6.1.4 Test Automation > 5.1.6.1.4.2 Formal Requirements Testing
content_sample: Automation test cases for requirements testing will be developed and run for formal HAL 4.0 MR1 verification using FAST framework, and Python scripting language. In addition to the formal TFS test case review and approval process, the associated automation test scripts are code reviewed by representatives from the automation team and Halcyon PVE team to ensure that acceptance criteria is met, and sufficient objective evidence is referenced and logged. The results of this code review is recorded within TFS and has a unique review ID. The automation script code is checked in after successful completion of code review. Results of the automation runs will be recorded in TFS in the same test plan area as manual tests. Automated test runs will include the test environment information for the run, a pass/fail evaluation for each test step along with the results of that step, any supporting objective

content_sample: evidence required, and an overall pass/fail evaluation for the test case (automated test runs will have the same information in TFS compared to manual test execution). The FAST automation framework along with the interface to TFS will be validated as a process tool prior to formal verification testing using test automation.

heading: 4.0 MR1 > 5.1.7 Environmental Needs
content_sample: This section identifies the necessary and desired test environments for verification testing, to ensure they are in place in time for the start of testing. Simulated use conditions are justified in the table below. Maintenance of the validation environment follows the process outlined in DND0913. For the Validation Environment refer to D70753 HAL 4.0 MR1 Validation Plan Rev 01, section 3 Environmental Needs. Note: Cell machines will be configuration controlled using SAP to ensure production equivalence. Formal test environments will have associated Test System Configuration Lists (TSCL) documentation. ID Test Environment DescriptionJustification for Simulated Use Conditions Cell A (SN3) Palo Alto Test Cell. Complete Halcyon 4.0 MR1 configured with old imager - (including covers and couch) capable of delivering MV and KV beam.  Access to ARIA database. No Sim and Treat capability. Device is production equivalent.  A phantom (or nothing) is

heading: 4.0 MR1 > 5.1.7 Environmental Needs (Continued)
content_sample: used in lieu of a patient during beam delivery.  Environment is suitable for validation and verification testing. Cell B (SN6) Baden Test Cell.  Complete Halcyon 4.0 MR1 (including covers and couch) capable of delivering MV and KV beam using advanced Imaging.  Capable of Sim and Treat workflow. (Machine has capability to switch between old and new KV imager). Device is production equivalent.  A phantom (or nothing) is used in lieu of a patient during beam delivery. Environment is suitable for validation and verification testing. Cell C (SN9) Baden Test Cell.  Complete Halcyon 4.0 MR1 (including covers and couch) capable of delivering MV and KV beam using advanced Imaging.  Capable of Sim and Treat workflow. (Machine has capability to switch between old and new KV imager). Device is production equivalent.  A phantom (or nothing) is used in lieu of a patient during beam delivery. Environment is suitable for validation and verification testing. Lab A (SQE1, SW1)

heading: 4.0 MR1 > 5.1.7 Environmental Needs (Continued)
content_sample: Palo Alto software lab (2):  Halcyon workstation and IRM computer and displays, with live view camera, CBCT reconstructor, XI imaging node, and Supervisor node. The Lab workstation is configured as production equivalent, including its interfaces to the supervisor node. The supervisor is simulating its interaction to other machine nodes (with the exception of imaging node).  Platform is suitable for verification of functional requirements of TXA, non-clinical mode apps, and IRM not directly associated with beam output, imaging, or beam quality. Lab B (SQE2, NLS2) Palo Alto software lab (3):  Halcyon workstation, Supervisor node, and XI imaging node. The Lab workstation is configured as production equivalent, including its interfaces to the supervisor node. The supervisor is simulating its interaction to other machine nodes (with the exception of imaging node).  Platform is suitable for verification of functional requirements of TXA,

heading: 4.0 MR1 > 5.1.7 Environmental Needs (Continued)
content_sample: non-clinical mode apps, not directly associated with beam output, imaging, live view camera, or beam quality. Also suitable for NLS testing. U/X Lab (UX2) Palo Alto U/X lab:  Halcyon workstation with DKB and Panel displays, Panel input devices, couch, bore collision detection, Supervisor node, STN node, Couch node, BGM node, pendant, CT reconstructor workstation, lasers, live view camera. U/X lab has production equivalent couch hardware, suitable for mechanical verification testing.  It also has production workstation, supervisor, STN, and user input devices, and is suitable for workflow testing involving patient positioning (align, load, shift, unload). Platform is suitable for verification of requirements not directly related to beam output, imaging, or beam quality.
